4:I[2228,["2972","static/chunks/2972-9ebc76942f6ff386.js","8574","static/chunks/app/blog/how-to-use-ai-workout-generator/page-2a7ea19672877a5d.js"],"default"]

c:I[2119,["2972","static/chunks/2972-9ebc76942f6ff386.js","570","static/chunks/570-b6ab106c455d39fe.js","3185","static/chunks/app/layout-2d5210fa376b13dc.js"],"Nav"]
d:I[376,["7601","static/chunks/app/error-00bcbcbf5966d127.js"],"default"]
2:Tb3c,{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"FAQPage","mainEntity":[{"@type":"Question","name":"What is an AI workout generator?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"An AI workout generator is a software tool that uses artificial intelligence and algorithms to create personalized exercise plans based on your goals, fitness level, available equipment, and schedule. It analyzes exercise science principles like progressive overload, muscle group balancing, and recovery to generate effective routines in seconds."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"Are AI-generated workouts effective?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Yes, AI-generated workouts are effective when used correctly. Research shows that well-designed AI workout plans produce comparable results to trainer-designed programs for most fitness goals. The key is providing accurate inputs about your experience level and equipment, then following the plan consistently with proper form and progressive overload."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"How often should I generate a new workout plan?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Stick with each AI-generated plan for 3-4 weeks before making major changes. This gives your body time to adapt and allows you to track progress. Minor adjustments can be made weekly based on how you feel. Changing plans too frequently prevents you from achieving progressive overload, which is essential for results."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"What information should I provide to an AI workout generator?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Provide accurate information about your fitness goal (fat loss, muscle building, strength, endurance), experience level (beginner, intermediate, advanced), available equipment (full gym, dumbbells only, bodyweight, etc.), training schedule (how many days per week), and any injuries or limitations. The more accurate your inputs, the better your plan will be."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"Can beginners use AI workout generators safely?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Yes, beginners can safely use AI workout generators by selecting the beginner experience level and following video demonstrations for exercise form. Start with lighter weights than you think you need, focus on learning proper movement patterns, and never sacrifice form for weight. If an exercise causes pain (not muscle burn), stop immediately."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"Do AI workout generators include warm-ups and cool-downs?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Most quality AI workout generators include warm-up and cool-down recommendations, but you should always verify this. A proper warm-up (5-10 minutes of light cardio and dynamic stretching) prepares your body for exercise and reduces injury risk. A cool-down with static stretching improves flexibility and aids recovery. Never skip these components."}}]}7:{"d":"M5 12h14","key":"1ays0h"}

9:{"d":"m12 5 7 7-7 7","key":"xquz4c"}

0:["nra-V8Q2Dq9acJyK7Fpeq",[[["",{"children":["blog",{"children":["how-to-use-ai-workout-generator",{"children":["__PAGE__",{}]}]}]},"$undefined","$undefined",true],["",{"children":["blog",{"children":["how-to-use-ai-workout-generator",{"children":["__PAGE__",{},[["$L1",["$","main",null,{"className":"min-h-screen bg-slate-950","children":[["$","script",null,{"type":"application/ld+json","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"{\"@context\":\"https://schema.org\",\"@type\":\"Article\",\"headline\":\"How to Use an AI Workout Generator: The Complete 2026 Guide\",\"description\":\"Learn how to get the most out of AI workout generators. Step-by-step guide to creating personalized training plans that actually work. Includes tips, mistakes to avoid, and best practices.\",\"image\":\"https://www.getfitai.io/images/og-ai-workout-guide.png\",\"author\":{\"@type\":\"Organization\",\"name\":\"GetFitAI\",\"url\":\"https://www.getfitai.io\"},\"publisher\":{\"@type\":\"Organization\",\"name\":\"GetFitAI\",\"logo\":{\"@type\":\"ImageObject\",\"url\":\"https://www.getfitai.io/logo.png\"}},\"datePublished\":\"2026-05-30\",\"dateModified\":\"2026-05-30\",\"mainEntityOfPage\":{\"@type\":\"WebPage\",\"@id\":\"https://www.getfitai.io/blog/how-to-use-ai-workout-generator\"}}"}}],["$","script",null,{"type":"application/ld+json","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$2"}}],["$","article",null,{"className":"pt-32 pb-20 px-4 sm:px-6","children":["$","div",null,{"className":"max-w-3xl mx-auto","children":[["$","header",null,{"className":"mb-12","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"flex items-center gap-3 mb-4","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"px-3 py-1 bg-orange-500/10 text-orange-400 text-xs font-medium rounded-full","children":"Guide"}],["$","span",null,{"className":"text-sm text-slate-500","children":"May 30, 2026"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"relative w-full h-64 md:h-80 rounded-2xl overflow-hidden mb-8","children":["$","img",null,{"src":"/images/blog/ai-workout-generator.jpg","alt":"Person using AI workout generator on smartphone at gym","className":"w-full h-full object-cover grayscale"}]}],["$","h1",null,{"className":"text-4xl md:text-5xl font-bold text-white mb-6 leading-tight","children":"How to Use an AI Workout Generator: The Complete 2026 Guide"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xl text-slate-400 leading-relaxed","children":"AI workout generators can create personalized training plans in seconds. But to get real results, you need to use them correctly. This step-by-step guide shows you exactly how to maximize your AI-generated workouts."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-slate-900/60 backdrop-blur-sm border border-slate-800 rounded-2xl p-6 md:p-8 mb-12","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"text-lg font-bold text-white mb-2","children":"Try Our Free AI Workout Generator"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-slate-400 mb-4","children":"Put this guide into practice immediately. Generate your personalized workout plan in under 30 seconds."}],["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/workouts/home","className":"inline-flex items-center gap-2 px-6 py-3 bg-gradient-to-r from-orange-500 to-orange-600 text-white rounded-full font-medium hover:from-orange-600 hover:to-orange-700 transition-all","children":["Generate My Workout",["$","$L4",null,{"iconNode":[["path",{"d":"M5 12h14","key":"1ays0h"}],["path",{"d":"m12 5 7 7-7 7","key":"xquz4c"}]],"className":"lucide-arrow-right w-4 h-4"}]]}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"prose prose-lg max-w-none prose-headings:text-white prose-p:text-slate-400 prose-strong:text-white prose-li:text-slate-400","children":[["$","h2",null,{"children":"Introduction: Why AI Workout Generators Are Game-Changers"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Creating an effective workout plan used to require hiring a personal trainer, spending hours researching exercise science, or following generic programs that weren't designed for your body. AI workout generators have changed the equation entirely."}],["$","p",null,{"children":"These tools use machine learning algorithms trained on millions of data points to create personalized training plans in seconds. They consider your goals, experience level, equipment, schedule, and even recovery needs. But like any tool, they only work if you know how to use them properly."}],["$","p",null,{"children":"In this guide, you'll learn the exact steps to get the most out of any AI workout generator, common mistakes that sabotage results, and advanced tips for optimizing your AI-generated plans."}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Step 1: Be Honest About Your Experience Level"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"This is the most critical step — and the one most people get wrong. AI workout generators adjust volume, intensity, exercise selection, and progression based on the experience level you select."}],["$","p",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Selecting \"advanced\" when you're a beginner"}]," leads to unrealistic plans, excessive soreness, potential injury, and eventual burnout. You'll receive exercises you can't perform correctly, volumes your body isn't ready for, and intensity levels that hinder rather than help progress."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["$","strong",null,{"children":"Here's how to accurately assess your level:"}]}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Beginner:"}]," You've been training consistently for less than 6 months, or you're returning after a long break. Focus on learning movement patterns and building a base."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Intermediate:"}]," You've been training consistently for 6 months to 2 years. You know proper form for major exercises and have established baseline strength."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Advanced:"}]," You've been training consistently for 2+ years. You've hit strength milestones and understand periodization, RPE, and advanced techniques."]}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":"When in doubt, choose the lower level. You can always increase intensity, but recovering from injury or burnout takes much longer."}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Step 2: Define Clear, Specific Goals"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Vague goals produce vague results. AI generators need specific inputs to create effective plans. Here are the most common goals and what they mean for your training:"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Build muscle (hypertrophy):"}]," Plans focus on moderate weights (60-80% of one-rep max), higher volume (3-5 sets of 8-12 reps), and shorter rest periods (60-90 seconds). Expect more isolation exercises and time-under-tension techniques."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Lose fat:"}]," Plans combine resistance training with cardio integration, higher overall volume, shorter rest periods to keep heart rate elevated, and full-body routines to maximize calorie burn."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Increase strength:"}]," Plans use heavier weights (80-90% of one-rep max), lower reps (3-6 per set), longer rest periods (2-5 minutes), and focus on compound movements like squats, deadlifts, and presses."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Improve endurance:"}]," Plans feature circuit-style training, higher rep ranges (15+), minimal rest between exercises, and progressive increases in workout duration."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"General fitness:"}]," Balanced approach combining elements of strength, cardio, and mobility. Great for overall health and maintenance."]}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Be specific about your timeline too. A 12-week muscle-building plan looks different from a 4-week fat-loss blitz."}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Step 3: Specify Your Equipment Accurately"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"This step is crucial for plan feasibility. A gym-based plan won't work if you only have dumbbells at home, and a bodyweight plan won't maximize your potential if you have access to a full gym."}],["$","p",null,{"children":["$","strong",null,{"children":"Common equipment options in AI generators:"}]}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Full gym access:"}]," Barbells, squat racks, cable machines, dumbbells, benches, cardio equipment — the works."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Dumbbells only:"}]," Great for home workouts. AI will select dumbbell variations of exercises and creative unilateral movements."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Bodyweight only:"}]," No equipment needed. AI focuses on calisthenics, plyometrics, and creative bodyweight progressions."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Resistance bands:"}]," Portable and versatile. AI uses band tension for resistance and includes band-specific exercises."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Minimal equipment:"}]," Usually means a pair of dumbbells and maybe a bench or pull-up bar."]}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Pro tip:"}]," If you have access to a gym but prefer home workouts, generate plans for your home setup. Consistency at home beats sporadic gym visits."]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Step 4: Set a Realistic Training Schedule"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Be brutally honest about how many days you can realistically train. Consistency beats intensity every time. A 3-day plan you follow religiously for 6 months produces better results than a 6-day plan you abandon after 3 weeks."}],["$","p",null,{"children":["$","strong",null,{"children":"Schedule guidelines:"}]}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"2 days/week:"}]," Full-body sessions each workout. Best for busy schedules or beginners."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"3 days/week:"}]," Full-body or upper/lower/full split. The sweet spot for most people."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"4 days/week:"}]," Upper/lower split or push/pull. Great for intermediate lifters with consistent schedules."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"5 days/week:"}]," Push/pull/legs/upper/lower or body part splits. For dedicated intermediate to advanced trainees."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"6 days/week:"}]," Push/pull/legs twice weekly or specialized programs. Only for advanced lifters with excellent recovery."]}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Remember: your muscles grow during recovery, not during workouts. Schedule at least 1-2 rest days per week."}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Step 5: Follow the Plan (But Listen to Your Body)"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"AI plans are data-driven, but they can't feel your fatigue or spot emerging injuries. There's a difference between productive discomfort (muscle burn, breathlessness) and dangerous pain (sharp joint pain, dizziness, chest tightness)."}],["$","p",null,{"children":["$","strong",null,{"children":"Green lights — push through:"}]}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":"Muscle burn during the last few reps"}],["$","li",null,{"children":"General fatigue and breathlessness"}],["$","li",null,{"children":"Mild muscle soreness (DOMS) the day after"}],["$","li",null,{"children":"Mental resistance (this is often when growth happens)"}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["$","strong",null,{"children":"Red lights — stop immediately:"}]}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":"Sharp or stabbing pain in joints"}],["$","li",null,{"children":"Pain that worsens during the movement"}],["$","li",null,{"children":"Dizziness, nausea, or chest pain"}],["$","li",null,{"children":"Numbness or tingling"}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":"If something hurts, stop the exercise and consult the AI generator for a modification, or seek professional medical advice if pain persists."}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Step 6: Track Your Progress and Adjust"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"After 2-3 weeks, assess your progress objectively. AI generators improve when you provide feedback, but you need data to give meaningful input."}],["$","p",null,{"children":["$","strong",null,{"children":"Track these metrics:"}]}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Strength:"}]," Weights used, reps completed, RPE (rate of perceived exertion)"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Body composition:"}]," Weight, measurements, progress photos (weekly)"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Performance:"}]," Workout duration, rest periods, energy levels"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Recovery:"}]," Sleep quality, soreness levels, motivation"]}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["$","strong",null,{"children":"Ask yourself:"}]}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":"Are you making progress toward your goal?"}],["$","li",null,{"children":"Is the volume too high (constant exhaustion) or too low (not challenged)?"}],["$","li",null,{"children":"Are you recovering adequately between sessions?"}],["$","li",null,{"children":"Do you enjoy the workouts enough to stick with them?"}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Most AI generators let you regenerate plans with adjusted parameters. Use this feature rather than abandoning the program entirely."}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Common Mistakes to Avoid"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Even with a great AI generator, these mistakes can derail your progress:"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Changing plans too often:"}]," Give each plan 3-4 weeks before major changes. Your body needs time to adapt."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Ignoring warm-ups:"}]," AI plans include warm-ups for a reason. Cold muscles are injury-prone muscles."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Skipping rest days:"}]," Recovery is when progress happens. Overtraining leads to plateaus and burnout."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Not tracking weights:"}]," Progressive overload requires data. You can't progress what you don't measure."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Ego lifting:"}]," Using weights too heavy for proper form. This leads to injury and stalls long-term progress."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Inconsistent inputs:"}]," Changing your goal or experience level every week confuses the AI and produces disjointed plans."]}]]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Advanced Tips for Maximizing AI Workouts"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Once you've mastered the basics, use these advanced strategies:"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Periodize your AI plans:"}]," Generate 4-week blocks with different focuses (hypertrophy → strength → deload → repeat)."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Use multiple generators:"}]," Compare plans from different AI tools to identify common exercises (these are likely most effective)."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Integrate wearable data:"}]," Some AI generators accept heart rate, HRV, and sleep data for better recovery management."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Customize rest periods:"}]," If the default rest feels too long or short, adjust based on your recovery needs."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Superset strategically:"}]," Pair non-competing exercises to save time without sacrificing performance."]}]]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Conclusion: Your AI Workout Journey Starts Now"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"AI workout generators are powerful tools, but they're not magic. They provide the roadmap — you still need to do the driving. Success comes from honest inputs, consistent execution, smart adjustments, and patience."}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Start by selecting the right experience level, defining clear goals, specifying your equipment, and committing to a realistic schedule. Track your progress, listen to your body, and adjust as needed. Avoid common mistakes like changing plans too frequently or skipping rest days."}],["$","p",null,{"children":"The best workout plan is the one you'll actually follow. AI generators remove the guesswork and planning burden, letting you focus on what matters: showing up and putting in the work."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"mt-16 relative overflow-hidden rounded-2xl","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"absolute inset-0 bg-gradient-to-br from-orange-500 via-orange-500 to-orange-600"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"absolute inset-0 bg-[radial-gradient(ellipse_at_top_right,_var(--tw-gradient-stops))] from-yellow-400/20 via-transparent to-transparent"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"absolute inset-0 mb-4","children":["Generate Your Personalized",["$","br",null,{"className":"hidden md:block"}],"Workout Plan Now"]}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-white/80 mb-8 max-w-xl mx-auto text-base md:text-lg","children":"Apply everything you learned in this guide. Create your custom AI workout plan in under 30 seconds."}],["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/workouts/home","className":"inline-flex items-center gap-2 px-8 py-4 bg-white text-orange-600 rounded-full font-bold hover:bg-slate-100 transition-colors shadow-xl shadow-black/20","children":["Generate My Plan",["$","$L4",null,{"iconNode":"$5","className":"lucide-arrow-right w-5 h-5"}]]}]]}]]}],["$","section",null,{"className":"mt-16","children":[["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-2xl font-bold text-white mb-8","children":"Frequently Asked Questions"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"space-y-6","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-slate-900/60 backdrop-blur-sm border border-slate-800 rounded-xl p-6","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-bold text-white mb-2","children":"What is an AI workout generator?"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-slate-400","children":"An AI workout generator is a software tool that uses artificial intelligence and algorithms to create personalized exercise plans based on your goals, fitness level, available equipment, and schedule. It analyzes exercise science principles like progressive overload, muscle group balancing, and recovery to generate effective routines in seconds."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-slate-900/60 backdrop-blur-sm border border-slate-800 rounded-xl p-6","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-bold text-white mb-2","children":"Are AI-generated workouts effective?"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-slate-400","children":"Yes, AI-generated workouts are effective when used correctly. Research shows that well-designed AI workout plans produce comparable results to trainer-designed programs for most fitness goals. The key is providing accurate inputs about your experience level and equipment, then following the plan consistently with proper form and progressive overload."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-slate-900/60 backdrop-blur-sm border border-slate-800 rounded-xl p-6","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-bold text-white mb-2","children":"How often should I generate a new workout plan?"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-slate-400","children":"Stick with each AI-generated plan for 3-4 weeks before making major changes. This gives your body time to adapt and allows you to track progress. Minor adjustments can be made weekly based on how you feel. Changing plans too frequently prevents you from achieving progressive overload, which is essential for results."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-slate-900/60 backdrop-blur-sm border border-slate-800 rounded-xl p-6","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-bold text-white mb-2","children":"What information should I provide to an AI workout generator?"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-slate-400","children":"Provide accurate information about your fitness goal (fat loss, muscle building, strength, endurance), experience level (beginner, intermediate, advanced), available equipment (full gym, dumbbells only, bodyweight, etc.), training schedule (how many days per week), and any injuries or limitations. The more accurate your inputs, the better your plan will be."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-slate-900/60 backdrop-blur-sm border border-slate-800 rounded-xl p-6","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-bold text-white mb-2","children":"Can beginners use AI workout generators safely?"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-slate-400","children":"Yes, beginners can safely use AI workout generators by selecting the beginner experience level and following video demonstrations for exercise form. Start with lighter weights than you think you need, focus on learning proper movement patterns, and never sacrifice form for weight. If an exercise causes pain (not muscle burn), stop immediately."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-slate-900/60 backdrop-blur-sm border border-slate-800 rounded-xl p-6","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-bold text-white mb-2","children":"Do AI workout generators include warm-ups and cool-downs?"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-slate-400","children":"Most quality AI workout generators include warm-up and cool-down recommendations, but you should always verify this. A proper warm-up (5-10 minutes of light cardio and dynamic stretching) prepares your body for exercise and reduces injury risk. A cool-down with static stretching improves flexibility and aids recovery. Never skip these components."}]]}]]}]]}]]}]}]]}],null],null],null]},[null,["$","$La",null,{"parallelRouterKey":"children","segmentPath":["children","blog","children","how-to-use-ai-workout-generator","children"],"error":"$undefined","errorStyles":"$undefined","errorScripts":"$undefined","template":["$","$Lb",null,{}],"templateStyles":"$undefined","templateScripts":"$undefined","notFound":"$undefined","notFoundStyles":"$undefined"}]],null]},[null,["$","$La",null,{"parallelRouterKey":"children","segmentPath":["children","blog","children"],"error":"$undefined","errorStyles":"$undefined","errorScripts":"$undefined","template":["$","$Lb",null,{}],"templateStyles":"$undefined","templateScripts":"$undefined","notFound":"$undefined","notFoundStyles":"$undefined"}]],null]},[[[["$","link","0",{"rel":"stylesheet","href":"/_next/static/css/08337a2139cd8e6c.css","precedence":"next","crossOrigin":"$undefined"}],["$","link","1",{"rel":"stylesheet","href":"/_next/static/css/be3aed8f55a64b30.css","precedence":"next","crossOrigin":"$undefined"}]],["$","html",null,{"lang":"en","className":"__variable_f367f3
